paper_metadata.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce a framework that abstracts Reinforcement Learning (RL) as a sequence modeling problem. This allows us to draw upon the simplicity and scalability of the Transformer architecture, and associated advances in language modeling such as GPT-x and BERT. In particular, we present Decision Transformer, an architecture that casts the problem of RL as conditional sequence modeling. Unlike prior approaches to RL that fit value functions or compute policy gradients, Decision Transformer simply outputs the optimal actions by leveraging a causally masked Transformer. By conditioning an autoregressive model on the desired return (reward), past states, and actions, our Decision Transformer model can generate future actions that achieve the desired return. Despite its simplicity, Decision Transformer matches or exceeds the performance of state-of-the-art model-free offline RL baselines on Atari, OpenAI Gym, and Key-to-Door tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: Decision Transformer reformulates reinforcement learning as a sequence modeling problem, using a causal Transformer to predict optimal actions conditioned on desired returns, past states, and actions, eliminating the need for dynamic programming or value functions.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Autoregressive sequence modeling of states, actions, and returns-to-go
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: OpenAI Gym datasets (HalfCheetah, Hopper, Walker, Reacher), Atari DQN-replay datasets, Graph random walk trajectories
extraction_data.extension: Modality-specific linear embeddings and positional episodic timestep encoding for states, actions, and returns-to-go, enabling autoregressive action prediction via causal self-attention masking.
extraction_data.research_problem: Offline Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
extraction_data.application: Policy optimization, trajectory generation, reinforcement learning tasks (Atari, OpenAI Gym, Key-to-Door)
extraction_data.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
